title: Rohinton Mistry

infobox.value: [University of Bombay](/wiki/University_of_Bombay "University of Bombay")  [University of Toronto](/wiki/University_of_Toronto "University of Toronto")

Historical fiction, [Postcolonial Literature](/wiki/Postcolonial_Literature "Postcolonial Literature"), [Realism](/wiki/Literary_realism "Literary realism"), Parsi Literature Minor Literature [Indian literature](/wiki/Indian_literature "Indian literature")

*[Such a Long Journey](/wiki/Such_a_Long_Journey_(novel) "Such a Long Journey (novel)")*; *[Family Matters](/wiki/Family_Matters_(novel) "Family Matters (novel)")*; *[A Fine Balance](/wiki/A_Fine_Balance "A Fine Balance")*

[CM](/wiki/Order_of_Canada "Order of Canada") (born 1952) is an [Indian](/wiki/India "India")-born [Canadian](/wiki/Canadians "Canadians") writer. He has been the recipient of the [Neustadt International Prize for Literature](/wiki/Neustadt_International_Prize_for_Literature "Neustadt International Prize for Literature") in 2012. Each of his three novels was [shortlisted](/wiki/List_of_winners_and_shortlisted_authors_of_the_Booker_Prize "List of winners and shortlisted authors of the Booker Prize") for the [Booker Prize](/wiki/Booker_Prize "Booker Prize"). All have been set in India, told from the perspective of [Parsis](/wiki/Parsis "Parsis"), and explore themes of family life, poverty, discrimination, and the corrupting influence of society.[[1]](#cite_note-1)
Mitchell, Tom (2000). ["Mistry, Rohinton"](https://scholarblogs.emory.edu/postcolonialstudies/2014/06/11/mistry-rohinton/). *Postcolonial Studies @ Emory*. Retrieved 19 May 2021.

Gupta, Nidhi (January 2014). ["The Making of the Novelist: Rohinton Mistry"](https://web.archive.org/web/20180422010043/http://mitpublications.org/yellow_images/1387359850_logo_3%20The%20Making%20of%20the%20Novelist%20Rohinton%20Mistry.pdf) (PDF). *MIT International Journal of English Language & Literature*. **1** (1): 1. Archived from [the original](http://www.mitpublications.org/yellow_images/1387359850_logo_3%20The%20Making%20of%20the%20Novelist%20Rohinton%20Mistry.pdf) (PDF) on 22 April 2018. Retrieved 19 February 2018.

["St. Xavier's College alumni write open letter opposing invitation to Aditya Thackeray to attend college event"](https://www.timesnownews.com/india/article/aditya-thackeray-st-xaviers-college-mumbai-alumni-shiv-sena-xaviers-college-invitation-150-years-anniversary-celebrations-rohinton-mistry-such-a-long/294150). *www.timesnownews.com*. 4 October 2018. Retrieved 21 July 2020.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Indian-born Canadian writer

| Rohinton Mistry   [CM](/wiki/Order_of_Canada "Order of Canada") | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | Rohinton Mistry  1952 (age 73–74)  [Bombay](/wiki/Bombay "Bombay"), India |
| Occupation | Novelist |
| Nationality | Canadian |
| Alma mater | [University of Bombay](/wiki/University_of_Bombay "University of Bombay")  [University of Toronto](/wiki/University_of_Toronto "University of Toronto") |
| Genre | Historical fiction, [Postcolonial Literature](/wiki/Postcolonial_Literature "Postcolonial Literature"), [Realism](/wiki/Literary_realism "Literary realism"), Parsi Literature Minor Literature [Indian literature](/wiki/Indian_literature "Indian literature") |
| Notable works | *[Such a Long Journey](/wiki/Such_a_Long_Journey_(novel) "Such a Long Journey (novel)")*; *[Family Matters](/wiki/Family_Matters_(novel) "Family Matters (novel)")*; *[A Fine Balance](/wiki/A_Fine_Balance "A Fine Balance")* |
| Spouse | Freny Elavia |

**Rohinton Mistry** [CM](/wiki/Order_of_Canada "Order of Canada") (born 1952) is an [Indian](/wiki/India "India")-born [Canadian](/wiki/Canadians "Canadians") writer. He has been the recipient of the [Neustadt International Prize for Literature](/wiki/Neustadt_International_Prize_for_Literature "Neustadt International Prize for Literature") in 2012. Each of his three novels was [shortlisted](/wiki/List_of_winners_and_shortlisted_authors_of_the_Booker_Prize "List of winners and shortlisted authors of the Booker Prize") for the [Booker Prize](/wiki/Booker_Prize "Booker Prize"). All have been set in India, told from the perspective of [Parsis](/wiki/Parsis "Parsis"), and explore themes of family life, poverty, discrimination, and the corrupting influence of society.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rohinton_Mistry&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Rohinton Mistry was born in [Bombay](/wiki/Bombay "Bombay"), India, to a [Parsi](/wiki/Parsi "Parsi") family.[[2]](#cite_note-2) His brother is the playwright and author [Cyrus Mistry](/wiki/Cyrus_Mistry_(writer) "Cyrus Mistry (writer)"). He earned a BA in Mathematics and Economics from [St. Xavier's College, Bombay](/wiki/St._Xavier%27s_College,_Bombay "St. Xavier's College, Bombay").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

He emigrated to Canada with his wife-to-be Freny Elavia in 1975 and they married shortly afterwards.[[4]](#cite_note-4) He worked in a bank for a while, before returning to academia at the [University of Toronto](/wiki/University_of_Toronto "University of Toronto") where he obtained a BA in English and Philosophy.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rohinton_Mistry&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This section needs to be **updated**. Please help update this article to reflect recent events or newly available information. *(October 2021)* |

While attending the [University of Toronto](/wiki/University_of_Toronto "University of Toronto") (Woodsworth College) he became the first to win two [Hart House](/wiki/Hart_House_(University_of_Toronto) "Hart House (University of Toronto)") literary prizes for stories published in the *[Hart House Review](/wiki/Hart_House_Review "Hart House Review")*, and *Canadian Fiction Magazine*'s annual Contributor's Prize for 1985.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Three years later, [Penguin Books](/wiki/Penguin_Books "Penguin Books") Canada published his collection of 11 [short stories](/wiki/Short_story "Short story"), *[Tales from Firozsha Baag](/wiki/Tales_from_Firozsha_Baag "Tales from Firozsha Baag")*. It was later published in the United States as *Swimming Lessons and Other Stories from Firozsha Baag*. The book consists of 11 stories set within an apartment complex in modern-day Bombay. This volume contains the oft-anthologized story, "Swimming Lessons".[[8]](#cite_note-bindu-8)

His second book, the novel *[Such a Long Journey](/wiki/Such_a_Long_Journey_(novel) "Such a Long Journey (novel)")*, was published in 1991. It won the [Governor General's Award](/wiki/Governor_General%27s_Award "Governor General's Award"), the [Commonwealth Writers Prize](/wiki/Commonwealth_Writers_Prize "Commonwealth Writers Prize") for Best Book, and the [W.H. Smith](/wiki/W.H._Smith "W.H. Smith")/[Books in Canada First Novel Award](/wiki/Books_in_Canada_First_Novel_Award "Books in Canada First Novel Award").[[8]](#cite_note-bindu-8) It was [shortlisted](/wiki/Shortlist "Shortlist") for the [Booker Prize](/wiki/Booker_Prize "Booker Prize") and for the [Trillium Award](/wiki/Trillium_Award "Trillium Award"). It has been translated into German, [Swedish](/wiki/Swedish_(language) "Swedish (language)"), [Norwegian](/wiki/Norwegian_language "Norwegian language"), [Danish](/wiki/Danish_language "Danish language") and Japanese. It was adapted for the 1998 film *[Such a Long Journey](/wiki/Such_a_Long_Journey_(film) "Such a Long Journey (film)")*. The content of the book caused controversy at [Mumbai University](/wiki/Mumbai_University "Mumbai University") in 2010 due to language used against [Bal Thackeray](/wiki/Bal_Thackeray "Bal Thackeray"), leader of [Shiv Sena](/wiki/Shiv_Sena_(1966%E2%80%932022) "Shiv Sena (1966–2022)"), a political party from [Maharashtra](/wiki/Maharashtra "Maharashtra"), as well as some remarks about [Maharashtrians](/wiki/Marathi_people "Marathi people").[[9]](#cite_note-9) The book was prescribed for the second year Bachelor of Arts (English) in 2007–08 as an optional text, according to University sources. Later, Dr. Rajan Welukar, [University of Mumbai](/wiki/University_of_Mumbai "University of Mumbai")'s Vice-Chancellor (V-C) used emergency powers in the Maharashtra Universities Act, 1994, to withdraw the book from the syllabus.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

His third book, and second novel, *[A Fine Balance](/wiki/A_Fine_Balance "A Fine Balance")* (1995), won the second annual [Giller Prize](/wiki/Giller_Prize "Giller Prize") in 1995, and the *[Los Angeles Times](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times "Los Angeles Times")* Book Prize for Fiction in 1996. It was selected for [Oprah's Book Club](/wiki/Oprah%27s_Book_Club "Oprah's Book Club")[[11]](#cite_note-11) in November 2001. It won the 1996 Commonwealth Writers Prize and was shortlisted for the 1996 Booker Prize.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

*[Family Matters](/wiki/Family_Matters_(novel) "Family Matters (novel)")* (2002) is a consideration of the difficulties that come with ageing, to which topic Mistry returned in 2006 with the short fiction *The Scream* (published as a separate volume, in support of World Literacy of Canada, with illustrations by [Tony Urquhart](/wiki/Tony_Urquhart "Tony Urquhart")). Mistry's literary papers are housed at the Clara Thomas Archives at [York University](/wiki/York_University "York University").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

In 2002, Mistry cancelled his United States book tour for his novel *Family Matters* after he and his wife were targeted by security agents at every airport.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)

## Awards and recognition

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rohinton_Mistry&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Awards and recognition")]

* 1983 – Hart House Literary Contest, "One Sunday"
* 1984 – Hart House Literary Contest, "Auspicious Occasion"
* 1985 – Annual Contributors' Prize, *Canadian Fiction Magazine*
* 1991 – [Booker Prize](/wiki/Booker_Prize "Booker Prize"), shortlist, *Such a Long Journey*
* 1991 – [Governor General's Award](/wiki/Governor_General%27s_Award "Governor General's Award"), *Such a Long Journey*
* 1991 – [Commonwealth Writers Prize](/wiki/Commonwealth_Writers_Prize "Commonwealth Writers Prize"), *Such a Long Journey*
* 1991 – [W.H. Smith](/wiki/W.H._Smith "W.H. Smith")/[Books in Canada First Novel Award](/wiki/Books_in_Canada_First_Novel_Award "Books in Canada First Novel Award"), *Such a Long Journey*
* 1991 – [Trillium Award](/wiki/Trillium_Award "Trillium Award"), *Such a Long Journey*
* 1995 – [Giller Prize](/wiki/Giller_Prize "Giller Prize"), *A Fine Balance*
* 1995 – [Los Angeles Times](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times "Los Angeles Times") Book Prize for Fiction, *A Fine Balance*
* 1996 – [Commonwealth Writers Prize](/wiki/Commonwealth_Writers_Prize "Commonwealth Writers Prize"), *A Fine Balance*
* 1996 – [Booker Prize](/wiki/Booker_Prize "Booker Prize"), shortlist, *A Fine Balance*
* 2002 – [Booker Prize](/wiki/Booker_Prize "Booker Prize"), shortlist, *Family Matters*
* 2002 – [James Tait Black Memorial Prize](/wiki/James_Tait_Black_Memorial_Prize "James Tait Black Memorial Prize"), shortlist *Family Matters*
* 2004 – [International Dublin Literary Award](/wiki/International_Dublin_Literary_Award "International Dublin Literary Award"), shortlist, *Family Matters*
* 2010 – Elected [Fellow of the Royal Society of Literature](/wiki/Royal_Society_of_Literature "Royal Society of Literature")[[16]](#cite_note-16)
* 2012 – [Neustadt International Prize for Literature](/wiki/Neustadt_International_Prize_for_Literature "Neustadt International Prize for Literature")[[17]](#cite_note-parsi-17)[[18]](#cite_note-rohinton-18)
* 2016 – Appointed as a [Member of the Order of Canada](/wiki/Member_of_the_Order_of_Canada "Member of the Order of Canada") (CM) in the [2016 Canadian honours](/wiki/2016_Canadian_honours "2016 Canadian honours")[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20)
* [Brampton Arts Walk of Fame](/wiki/Brampton_Arts_Walk_of_Fame "Brampton Arts Walk of Fame"), [Brampton](/wiki/Brampton "Brampton"), Ontario

## Selected works

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rohinton_Mistry&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Selected works")]

### Novels

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rohinton_Mistry&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Novels")]

* *[Such a Long Journey](/wiki/Such_a_Long_Journey_(novel) "Such a Long Journey (novel)")* (1991)
* *[A Fine Balance](/wiki/A_Fine_Balance "A Fine Balance")* (1995)
* *[Family Matters](/wiki/Family_Matters_(novel) "Family Matters (novel)")* (2002)[[21]](#cite_note-21)

### Short stories and chapbooks

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rohinton_Mistry&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Short stories and chapbooks")]

* *[Tales from Firozsha Baag](/wiki/Tales_from_Firozsha_Baag "Tales from Firozsha Baag")* (1987), also published as *Swimming Lessons and Other Stories from Firozsha Baag* (1989)
* *Searching for Stevenson* (1994)
* *The Scream* (2006)
